steps.title: Identify the time multiplier
steps.content:
The time changes from $5y$ minutes to $9y$ minutes. The ratio is:

$$\frac{9y}{5y} = \frac{9}{5}$$

So the time increases by a factor of $\frac{9}{5}$.

steps.title: Apply proportional reasoning to find the amount
steps.content:
Since the rate is constant, the amount of water is proportional to time. If time increases by a factor of $\frac{9}{5}$, so does the amount:

$$\text{New amount} = 88x \cdot \frac{9}{5} = \frac{792x}{5}$$

That matches choice D — D is correct.
